entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume crop studied for its ability to utilize phosphorus and release organic acids

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The essential nutrient whose solubilization and uptake are the focus of the study

entities.name: ALUMINIUM PHOSPHATE
entities.description: An insoluble source of phosphorus used in pot experiments

entities.name: IRON PHOSPHATE
entities.description: An insoluble source of phosphorus used in pot experiments

entities.name: APATITE
entities.description: An insoluble source of phosphorus used in pot experiments

entities.name: QUALITATIVE ASSAY METHOD
entities.description: A method used to detect the presence of iron-P solubilizing substances in root exudates

entities.name: IRON-P SOLUBILIZING ACTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of root exudates to solubilize iron-bound phosphorus

entities.name: ROOT EXUDATES
entities.description: Chemical substances secreted by the roots of pigeonpea into the rhizosphere

entities.name: P-DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A condition where the plant lacks sufficient phosphorus, triggering the release of solubilizing substances

entities.name: ROOT TIPS
entities.description: The anatomical region of the root where Fe-P solubilizing substances are primarily released

entities.name: CITRIC ACID
entities.description: An organic acid released by roots that is highly effective at mobilizing phosphorus from soil

entities.name: MALIC ACID
entities.description: An organic acid identified in the root exudates of pigeonpea

entities.name: MALONIC ACID
entities.description: An organic acid identified in the root exudates of pigeonpea

entities.name: SUCCINIC ACID
entities.description: An organic acid identified in the root exudates of pigeonpea

entities.name: PISCIDIC ACID
entities.description: An organic acid released by pigeonpea roots in significantly higher quantities than citric acid

entities.name: LOW-P TREATMENT
entities.description: An experimental condition of low phosphorus availability used to stimulate organic acid release

entities.name: P CONCENTRATION OF SHOOTS
entities.description: The level of phosphorus in the plant's aerial parts, which influences organic acid release

entities.name: P CONCENTRATION OF ROOTS
entities.description: The level of phosphorus in the plant's root system

entities.name: P STRESS STATUS
entities.description: The physiological state of the plant resulting from phosphorus deficiency

entities.name: PLANT AGE
entities.description: The developmental stage of the pigeonpea plant, affecting the release of piscidic acid

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic variation among pigeonpea varieties affecting phosphorus utilization and acid release

entities.name: ALFISOLS
entities.description: A specific soil type used to test the phosphorus mobilizing capability of organic acids

entities.name: HYDROPONIC CULTURE
entities.description: A soil-less growth medium used to study the release rates of organic acids

entities.name: P UPTAKE
entities.description: The process of absorbing phosphorus from the growth medium

entities.name: LOW P AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The environmental condition of limited phosphorus in the soil
